seo.title: Building AI Chatbot with Amazon Bedrock Agents + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai chatbot with Amazon Bedrock Agents + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Amazon Bedrock Agents enable enterprise-grade AI chatbots with built-in knowledge bases, action groups, and guardrails for secure conversational AI. Integrating Vex provides runtime verification to catch malformed responses, hallucination, and policy violations before they reach users. This ensures production reliability for high-risk customer-facing deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Chatbot Architecture with Amazon Bedrock Agents
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture centers on Bedrock Agents orchestrating multi-turn conversations with RAG from knowledge bases and Lambda action groups for external API calls. User input flows through optional guardrails, intent classification, then agent execution which retrieves context, reasons over tools, and generates responses. Session memory maintains conversation state across turns while PII filters and safety checks run in parallel.

Knowledge bases use vector embeddings for semantic search, action groups execute business logic via Lambda, and guardrails enforce content policies. The agent runtime handles orchestration, but failure modes like chunking issues or malformed Lambda responses require post-processing verification.

Vex wraps the invoke_agent() API to validate response accuracy, coherence, and safety before delivery, closing the reliability gap in production deployments.

risks.name: Malformed Lambda Action Responses
risks.description: Action group Lambda functions return invalid JSON or missing required fields, breaking agent orchestration
risks.mitigation: Wrap invoke_agent() with Vex schema validation on action outputs

risks.name: Knowledge Base Chunking Failures
risks.description: RAG retrieval misses relevant documents due to poor chunking strategy or embedding mismatches
risks.mitigation: Verify response-grounding with Vex hallucination detection against retrieved context

risks.name: Guardrail Bypass
risks.description: Domain-specific policy violations slip through generic Bedrock guardrails
risks.mitigation: Layer Vex custom safety filtering on agent responses

risks.name: Multi-turn State Loss
risks.description: Session memory fails to maintain conversation context across agent invocations
risks.mitigation: Validate conversation coherence with Vex state tracking

implementation.title: Basic Bedrock Agent Invocation (Python)
implementation.description: Core invoke_agent call with session management for multi-turn chat
implementation.code:
import boto3
import json

bedrock_agent = boto3.client('bedrock-agent-runtime')

agent_id = 'YOUR_AGENT_ID'
agent_alias_id = 'YOUR_AGENT_ALIAS_ID'
session_id = 'chat-session-123'

response = bedrock_agent.invoke_agent(
    agentId=agent_id,
    agentAliasId=agent_alias_id,
    sessionId=session_id,
    inputText='What is our return policy?',
    enableTrace=True
)

# Stream completion events
completion = ''
for event in response['completion']:
    if 'chunk' in event:
        completion += event['chunk']['bytes'].decode()

print(json.dumps({'response': completion}, indent=2))

vexIntegration.title: Python Vex Verification Wrapper
vexIntegration.description: Wrap invoke_agent() with comprehensive verification for all risk areas
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

from your_bedrock_client import invoke_bedrock_agent

@vex.workflow(
    name="bedrock-chatbot",
    risk_profile="high",
    verifications=[
        "response_accuracy",
        "conversation_coherence",
        "safety_filtering",
        "pii_protection",
        "hallucination_detection"
    ]
)
def verified_chatbot(input_text: str, session_id: str, context: dict = None) -> str:
    # Invoke Bedrock Agent
    raw_response = invoke_bedrock_agent(input_text, session_id)
    
    # Vex verifies response before returning
    verified_response = vex.verify(
        raw_response,
        context={
            'session_history': context,
            'domain': 'ecommerce_chatbot',
            'pii_patterns': ['order-\d+', 'card-\d+']
        }
    )
    
    return verified_response['content']
